title: Workday Employee Attrition Risk and Voluntary Turnover Prediction
dataSources: Primary: `index=business` with one or more Workday-sourced sourcetypes defined locally (not Splunkbase-shipped), for example `workday:rpt_active_workers` (RaaS JSON via custom modular input), `workday:wws_workers` (Workday Web Services SOAP `Get_Workers` with `Response_Group` paging), and/or `workday:rpt_terminations` (voluntary and involuntary rows with `Termination_Category`, `Termination_Reason`, `Manager_Worker_ID`). Each event should carry at minimum: `Worker_ID`, `Employee_ID`, `snapshot_date` or `As_of_Effective_Date`, `Active_Status`, `Hire_Date`, `Length_of_Service_in_Years`, `Compa_Ratio`, `Last_Salary_Increase_Date`, `Performance_Rating`, `Job_Family`, `Business_Unit`, `Region`, `Supervisory_Organization`, `Manager_Worker_ID`, `On_Leave`, `Worker_Status_Detail`. Optional parallel path: `index=business` `sourcetype=dbx:hris_workers` / `dbx:hris_terminations` from Splunk DB Connect (Splunkbase 2686) JDBC pulls against a Snowflake/Redshift/SQL Server HR warehouse where nightly ETL lands `dim_employee` and `fact_terminations` with column names aligned to the same logical fields. Document the exact sourcetype and field casing you chose in `props.conf` on the first parsing tier.
app: No canonical Splunkbase add-on ingests full Workday HRIS worker rows. This UC uses a custom app with (A) a Python modular input or `script://` poller for Workday RaaS (Report-as-a-Service) REST, or (B) a SOAP client for WWS `Human_Resources` v40.x+ against `https://wd<N>-services1.workday.com/ccx/service/<tenant>/...`, or (C) Splunk DB Connect ([Splunkbase 2686](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/2686)) to a warehouse mirror. HEC (built-in) receives rows from the poller. Optional: the Splunk add-on is only the combination of HEC and your `local/` app, not a first-party “Splunk_TA_Workday” on Splunkbase.
spl:
index=business (sourcetype="workday:rpt_active_workers" OR sourcetype="dbx:hris_workers") earliest=-1d@d latest=now
| eval _time=if(isnotnull(snapshot_date),strptime(snapshot_date,"%Y-%m-%d"), if(isnotnull(As_of_Effective_Date),strptime(As_of_Effective_Date,"%Y-%m-%d"), _time))
| where isnotnull(Worker_ID)
| eval Active_Status_norm=if(Active_Status=1 OR lower(tostring(Active_Status)) IN ("1","y","true","active"),1,0)
| where Active_Status_norm=1 AND (On_Leave=0 OR isnull(On_Leave) OR lower(tostring(On_Leave)) IN ("0","n","false")) AND (like(lower(tostring(Worker_Status_Detail)),"%active%") OR isnull(Worker_Status_Detail))
| dedup Worker_ID sortby -_time
| eval tenure_years=tonumber(Length_of_Service_in_Years)
| eval time_since_increase_days=if(isnotnull(Last_Salary_Increase_Date), (now() - strptime(Last_Salary_Increase_Date, "%Y-%m-%d"))/86400, null())
| eval comp_ratio=tonumber(Compa_Ratio)
| eval risk_tenure=case(tenure_years<1,3, tenure_years<2,2, tenure_years>=10 AND tenure_years<15,2, 1=1,1)
| eval risk_pay=case(comp_ratio<0.85,3, comp_ratio<0.95,2, 1=1,1)
| eval risk_promo=case(time_since_increase_days>730,3, time_since_increase_days>540,2, 1=1,1)
| eval risk_perf=case(Performance_Rating="Exceeds" AND risk_pay>=2,3, Performance_Rating="Below",2, 1=1,1)
| join type=left Manager_Worker_ID [ search index=business (sourcetype="workday:rpt_terminations" OR sourcetype="dbx:hris_terminations") earliest=-365d@d (Termination_Category="Voluntary" OR like(lower(tostring(Termination_Reason)),"voluntary%")) | rename Worker_ID as departed_Worker_ID | stats dc(departed_Worker_ID) as mgr_voluntary_loss_12m by Manager_Worker_ID ]
| eval risk_mgr=case(mgr_voluntary_loss_12m>=4,3, mgr_voluntary_loss_12m>=2,2, 1=1,1)
| eval risk_score=risk_tenure + risk_pay + risk_promo + risk_perf + risk_mgr
| eval risk_band=case(risk_score>=12,"Critical", risk_score>=9,"High", risk_score>=6,"Moderate", 1=1,"Low")
| stats dc(Worker_ID) as headcount sum(eval(if(risk_band="Critical",1,0))) as critical_risk sum(eval(if(risk_band="High",1,0))) as high_risk sum(eval(if(risk_band="Moderate",1,0))) as moderate_risk by Business_Unit, Region
| eval pct_critical=round(100*critical_risk/headcount,1) | eval pct_at_risk=round(100*(critical_risk+high_risk)/headcount,1)
| sort -pct_at_risk
description: It scores active Workday Worker_ID records using tenure, compa-ratio, time since the last pay change, Performance_Rating, and a manager’s trailing voluntary terminations, then groups Business_Unit and Region to show which populations carry the heaviest predicted voluntary-exit load. The same pipeline is meant for HRBP huddles and executive risk readouts, not a one-off static turnover report, because the SPL intentionally separates controllable voluntary drivers from one-off data-motion noise called out in the false-positive note.
value: People cost and business continuity: replacing mid-level individual contributors and managers typically runs tens to hundreds of thousands of dollars per exit in backfill, lost productivity, and retraining; surfacing Critical and High bands a quarter earlier lets you fund retention, manager coaching, and targeted comp review before the resignations show up in recruiting spend and revenue-at-risk metrics. The rollups also give workforce-planning and internal-audit functions a time-stamped evidence trail in Splunk that the CHRO and CFO can align on when they negotiate cost-of-workforce guardrails, without exporting raw PII to spreadsheets that fall outside DLP and GDPR Article 5 minimization policies.
implementation: (1) Choose ingestion path: RaaS JSON to HEC, WWS SOAP to HEC, or DB Connect to the warehouse; document sourcetype in `props.conf` because Workday does not ship a first-party TA; (2) land active-worker snapshots and termination history with `Termination_Category` and `Manager_Worker_ID` for the join; (3) run the `spl` daily after your snapshot lands (T-1 for effective-dated stability); (4) publish Dashboard Studio panels with `splunk.treemap` and `splunk.table` and route Critical concentration only to HR-scoped roles, not a generic org-wide index; (5) re-baseline the risk_score weights annually with HR data science; (6) add a `lookup` of excluded contractor classes if `Worker_ID` mix includes reclassified contingent workers.
visualization: (1) `splunk.treemap` of `headcount` with color by `pct_at_risk` for `Business_Unit` to show which segments dominate population-weighted exposure. (2) `splunk.table` of `Region`, `Business_Unit`, `headcount`, `critical_risk`, `high_risk`, `pct_at_risk` with a numeric sort on `pct_at_risk` for the weekly huddle. (3) Stacked `splunk.column` of risk-band counts by `Job_Family` (drill to Supervisory_Organization on token click) from a sibling panel using the pre-`stats` output. (4) `splunk.line` of trailing 8-week `mgr_voluntary_loss_12m` distribution (manager-decile) to show whether the issue is a few outlier people leaders versus broad-based pay compression. (5) `splunk.singlevalue` pair: `pct_at_risk` for the top Region versus enterprise average, with a static subtitle naming the as-of snapshot_date from the RaaS run.

references.title: Splunk — Use the HTTP Event Collector
Splunk DB Connect — install and system requirements
OECD — Guidelines on the Protection of Privacy and Transborder Flows of Personal Data (data minimization context for HR analytics)
EDPB — Guidelines 01/2020 on processing personal data in the context of connected vehicles and related applications (illustrates GDPR data minimization principle used for HR use cases)

knownFalsePositives: A supervisory-organization rename or a large M&A mapping exercise can shift `Supervisory_Organization` or `Business_Unit` on effective-dated rows so last week’s “spike” in predicted exit load is a geography label change, not a new behavioral signal—reconcile to Workday’s org-hierarchy effective dates before you fund interventions. A worker transferred with `Worker_Status_Detail=Transferred` is not a termination, but a thin snapshot that only reads `Active_Status=0` can look like a loss of headcount. Retirement rows often carry `Termination_Category` or reason taxonomies outside the Voluntary bucket; folding retirement into the voluntary model swells controllable High and Critical counts by 10–20% unless you keep a separate bucket. Integration System User pulls can accidentally mix system_user test accounts with human Worker_ID if domain security is too broad, which pollutes compa-ratio and manager-loss stats until you filter `Worker_Type` or worker-class fields at ingest.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• A Splunk `business` (or equivalent) index and a role with `srchIndexesAllowed` covering it. Heavy Forwarder or a supported execution host for a Python 3.9+ poller and outbound `443/tcp` to your Workday tenant host and to the Splunk HEC endpoint.
• Workday tenant identifiers for production versus preview (Sandbox): production hosts follow `https://wd<N>-services1.workday.com/...` while `wd<N>-impl-services1.workday.com/...` is the common implementation (preview) pattern—storing the wrong base URL in your modular input is a frequent source of data that is syntactically valid but refers to a non-prod tenant name.
• An Integration System User (ISU) in Workday, not a human Workday UI user. Grant domain access only for the report fields and WWS operations you need. Typical domains for a worker snapshot plus terminations: Worker Data: Public Worker Reports and whatever compensation domains your compa Compa_Ratio report actually reads (for example Worker Data: Compensation by Supervisory Organization), plus Get_Workers-related entitlements for WWS; missing domain entitlements return HTTP 200 with empty Report_Data or zero-row SOAP Response_Results—there is no SOAP Fault to grep when security is the root cause. Configure the ISU for SAML or WS-Security UsernameToken as your integration standard requires; many enterprises pair the ISU with a dedicated IdP or certificate rotation calendar.
• PII and GDPR Article 5: Do not ingest SSN, DOB, street address, or benefits payload fields unless legal sign-off and retention controls exist. If a report definition accidentally includes SSN, add `SEDCMD-*` redaction in `props.conf` on the forwarder or indexer tier, and file a Workday report change request to remove the field upstream.
• No canonical Splunk Add-on for Workday on Splunkbase. Workday does not publish a canonical Splunk Add-on; this UC uses one of three documented integration patterns: Workday RaaS, Workday Web Services (WWS) SOAP, or Splunk DB Connect against a HR data warehouse mirror. Sourcetypes such as `workday:workers`, `workday:terminations`, or `dbx:hris_workers` are HEC/scripted-input conventions defined per deployment, not Splunkbase-published. Document the chosen sourcetype in your local `props.conf`.

**Step 1 — Configure data collection**
Path A (RaaS): In **Workday RaaS**, publish a custom report (for example “Active workers — attrition model fields”) that emits **Worker_ID**, `Active_Status`, `Length_of_Service_in_Years`, `Compa_Ratio`, `Last_Salary_Increase_Date`, `Performance_Rating`, `Manager_Worker_ID`, `Job_Family`, `Business_Unit`, `Region`, `Supervisory_Organization`, `On_Leave`, `Worker_Status_Detail`, and `As_of_Effective_Date` (or a `snapshot_date` column you add in Workday Studio). If the service returns XML from XSLT rendering, your Python poller should transform XML to one JSON line per worker key before HEC. Expose the REST endpoint: `https://wd<N>-services1.workday.com/ccx/service/customreport2/<tenant>/<owner>/<report_name>?format=json` and authenticate the ISU with basic credentials or an OAuth 2.0 client you registered under View Setup → Integration Systems. Respect RaaS throttling: plan for on the order of five concurrent and roughly 60 report executions per ISU per clock minute at peak; for pulls above ~50,000 Worker rows, implement pagination (additional prompt filters) rather than a single unbounded run. Add `format=json` or `format=csv` consistently with the parser you wrote in the modular input, and set `sourcetype=workday:rpt_active_workers` in `inputs.conf` for that stanza. Point HEC to `https://<splunk>:8088` with a dedicated HEC token whose index is the `business` index.

Path B (WWS SOAP): Target the versioned Human_Resources endpoint (v40.0+ is typical in current tenants; pin the `v40.0` string to what your WSDL import shows in the integration catalog). Build a `Get_Workers` request with `Response_Group` elements that return Organization_Data, Employment_Information_Data, and Compensation_Data as required for Compa_Ratio; pass `Page` and `Count` (or equivalent pagination knobs) to loop because default page sizes are often 100 effective rows, and use `Total_Pages` in the SOAP response to terminate the loop. Set SOAP `Effective_As_Of_Date` to T-1 end-of-day if you need stable effective-dated worker facts for modeling. If you batch many Worker_ID values in one SOAP envelope, keep each batch under your integration timeout; split by region for parallel modular-input workers. Sourcetype `workday:wws_workers` is a reasonable convention. Use WS-Security UsernameToken with the ISU and handle SOAP faults when Workday returns Processing_Fault (non-zero error codes) — log the raw fault XML in your poller to `$SPLUNK_HOME/var/log/splunk/`, not to the business index, when debugging.

Path C (DB Connect): Install `splunk_app_db_connect` ([Splunkbase 2686](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/2686)) on a Heavy Forwarder and define a JDBC connection to Snowflake, Amazon Redshift, or SQL Server where your ETL from Workday lands `dim_employee` and `fact_terminations`. Use a `db_input` with a `WHERE` clause on the warehouse’s `as_of` or `snapshot` column so each scheduled run fetches a bounded delta. Sourcetype `dbx:hris_workers` and index `business` are common; name columns in the SQL projection to Worker_ID, Compa_Ratio, etc., so the top-level `spl` needs only minor `coalesce` adjustments. Expect T+1 latency for warehouse-backed facts.

**Step 2 — Create the search and alert**
Copy the `spl` field from this UC into Search and run over your chosen window. The join to terminations is intentionally limited to `Termination_Category` values that your HR taxonomy marks as voluntary; extend the `search` subfilter if your `Termination_Reason` string scheme nests “Voluntary > …” with different casing. Save as a Report (scheduled daily) and a separate Alert on `pct_critical` for a `Business_Unit` that exceeds a threshold only after a seven-day streak test from a `summary` or lookup-based backfill, so a single `snapshot_date` miss does not page leadership. Use throttle on `Business_Unit`+`Region` to avoid mail storms during a corporate event.

**Step 3 — Validate**
(a) In Workday Custom Reports, run the same RaaS report (or a material Report Writer view) for five Worker_ID values the SPL lists as Critical and compare Compa_Ratio and `Length_of_Service_in_Years` to what HR sees. (b) Compare enterprise active headcount from the deduped SPL to a headcount report in Workday with the same `As_of_Effective_Date` — variance usually traces to a wrong snapshot (today versus T-1) or on-leave filter gaps. (c) Reconcile the voluntary-termination subsearch dc to a Workday termination report for the last 12 months; if the Splunk subsearch is high, you included rehire or transfer events—tighten `Worker_Status_Detail`.

**Step 4 — Operationalize**
Build a Dashboard Studio board with the visualization mix in the `visualization` field: treemap, sortable `splunk.table`, stacked column, line on manager deciles, and a single-value pair for top Region versus mean. **Settings → Permissions** the dashboard to a HR-people-analytics app context with row-level `lookup` to strip PII from drilldown. Route email to CHRO office distribution lists only; avoid broadcasting individual worker key lists in the email body. For EU populations, set report acceleration on the saved report that backs the treemap only if the dataset is large enough; otherwise use loadjob-style scheduled PDF exports from the saved search. Pair this dashboard with a lightweight JIRA or ServiceNow HR intake queue when a Region stays above a High+Critical share for a month.

**Step 5 — Troubleshooting**
• Empty RaaS body but HTTP 200 — the ISU lacks Get on the report’s domain or the tenant in the `customreport2` URL is `acme` while the ISU is authorized only for `acme_implementation`. In Workday Maintain Authentication for Integration Systems, re-open the ISU, verify SAML metadata if you use it, and under Workday Domain Security grant the missing Worker Data report domains. In Splunk, run `| makeresults | eval u="https://wd5-services1.workday.com/...` only on a test box with secrets redacted, and compare the returned byte length to a browser download using a separate break-glass human session—do not post credentials into Splunk as events.
• SOAP VersionMismatch or MustUnderstand faults on WWS — your XML namespace for Human_Resources does not match the WSDL on disk; pull the current WSDL from the tenant’s `ccx/service/.../Human_Resources?wsdl` and update the `v40.0` path segment. SOAP 1.1 vs 1.2 is fixed per Workday binding; a wrong Content-Type (`text/xml; charset=utf-8` for SOAP 1.1) yields 500 with a generic faultstring; fix headers in the Python `requests` call before touching Splunk.
• XSLT RaaS report returns HTML — you toggled an XSLT transform in the report delivery that the poller does not expect; either remove the XSLT in Workday for the integration service account, or add an `lxml` transform stage in the modular input. Validate by downloading once to `curl` output to a file and opening in a text editor, not a browser, so you can see the root element.
• PII in Splunk — `rex` a sample of `| head 20` in a restricted role; if a SSN-shaped 9-digit string appears, immediately open a DLP plus Workday change ticket, add `SEDCMD-redact` on the sourcetype, and delete the index bucket window per your records-management policy. Under GDPR data-minimization, shrink the HEC payload to worker keys and model fields only.
• All workers land at `_time=now()` because `snapshot_date` is missing; `strptime` fails silently on nulls. Fix the RaaS column name or set `_time` from last successful poll in the input code so timechart does not show a spike at a single second.
• HEC 401 or 403 on the collector — wrong HEC token or the token is not enabled on the right indexer; confirm through **Settings → Data inputs → HTTP Event Collector** that the `Authorization` format is `Splunk <token>`. A load balancer stripping headers between the forwarder and Splunk Cloud is another common failure mode—test with `curl` from the Heavy Forwarder using `-v` to print HTTP status, not only the response body, on port 8088/tcp to the HEC `uri` the architecture diagram lists.
• JDBC (DB Connect) fetch returns fewer rows than Workday — the warehouse is stale after an ETL failure: query the warehouse’s `etl_load_id` and compare to the Fivetran/Matillion job history; the Splunk `dbx` run is not the first place to fix a missing `merge` in Snowflake but it is the first place the symptom appears.
• Over-counted voluntary `mgr_voluntary_loss_12m` — `Termination_Reason` strings map reduction in force as Voluntary because of a bad legacy mapping. Work with the Workday HCM business process owner to reclassify the pick-list and rebuild a lookup that maps `Termination_Reason` to a normalized controllable flag before the join runs.
grandmaExplanation: We look at who still works for the company, how fair their pay is compared to the job’s midpoint, how long it has been since a raise, and which managers have already lost several people on purpose, then add those signals up to say which big teams look shaky. It is a bit like checking which neighborhoods have had a lot of “for sale” signs before you find out the whole market moved overnight.
